name: Buckinghamshire (United Kingdom)
description: A red and white field divided per pale, charged with a chained white swan with raised wings.
history: Adopted in 2011 after a public competition; the swan derives from the Buckinghamshire county arms and the medieval badge of the de Bohun earls of Buckingham. Registered with the Flag Institute on 19 May 2011.

features.symbolism: the de Bohun swan, a heraldic badge associated with Buckinghamshire since the medieval period
